test.name: Multiple warnings on one person escalate to identity-confusion review
test.description: User asks to check Patrick. The tool returns three warnings on the same person: hasEventAfterDeath1 (error), hasAgeRangeGreaterThan120 (error), and tooManyDeathDates2 (warning). Per the cluster-escalation rule in warnings-as-identity-signals.md, '2+ errors → almost certainly two people merged.' The skill should NOT just list three warnings independently; it should explicitly recognize the cluster as a strong identity-confusion signal and recommend a timeline review + person_evidence rebuild.

input.user_message: Run a thorough warnings check on Patrick Flynn.

judge_context: Should call person_warnings on Patrick (I1) and report all three warnings
Should explicitly recognize the cluster (2+ errors + 1 warning on one person) as a near-certain identity-confusion signal — not just present three independent items
Should hand off to the timeline skill to find the split point, AND to person-evidence to rebuild the assertion-to-person links once the split is identified
Should classify the severities correctly: hasEventAfterDeath1 and hasAgeRangeGreaterThan120 as Critical (error), tooManyDeathDates2 as Note (warning)
Should NOT recommend fixing individual fields when the cluster suggests the whole profile is the wrong thing — fixing one date won't clear the underlying identity issue
Should reference the escalation rule from warnings-as-identity-signals.md (2+ errors → split the profile, rebuild person_evidence)
